# Inbox

> A privacy-first personal note-taking system with Telegram bot integration, voice transcription, and calendar sync. Self-hosted on Raspberry Pi -- no cloud required. Built with Ruby on Rails 8.1, SQLite3, Stimulus.js, and Tailwind CSS.

Inbox is a single-user, block-based note-taking app designed for privacy and local data ownership. It captures information from multiple sources (web editor, Telegram bot, voice recordings), processes it with local AI transcription (Parakeet v3 / onnx-asr), and stores everything in SQLite. The system runs entirely on a Raspberry Pi with Docker Compose.

Key capabilities:
- Telegram bot captures text, voice messages, photos, and files
- In-browser audio recording with automatic transcription via MediaRecorder API
- Local voice-to-text via Parakeet v3 (25 languages, no cloud API)
- Google Calendar sync with event import and Telegram reminders
- Tags, tasks with due dates, full-text search, Markdown editor
- Background jobs via SolidQueue / Sidekiq with Redis

## Documentation

- [README](README.md): Project overview, features, quick start (Docker), development setup, environment variables, architecture diagram, data flow
- [Contributing Guide](CONTRIBUTING.md): Development setup, running tests (RSpec), code style (RuboCop), security scanning (Brakeman), PR guidelines, Conventional Commits
- [Project Vision & About](.project/about.md): Vision, problem statement, goals, success criteria, target audience, value proposition, features, scope boundaries, technology stack, key decisions
- [Technical Architecture](.project/architecture.md): Full tech stack with versions, runtime & tooling, environment configuration, REST API endpoints & contracts, observability (logging, metrics, health checks), security requirements, quality standards, deployment & operations, performance budgets, finalized technical decisions
- [Technical Specifications](.project/specs.md): Version policy (locked versions), project summary
- [Deployment Quickstart](.project/deployment-quickstart.md): Production deployment on Raspberry Pi 5 with WireGuard VPN, nginx reverse proxy on Digital Ocean, Docker Compose, Telegram webhook registration, SSL via certbot, monitoring, backup strategy, troubleshooting

## Feature Specifications

- [Audio Recording Proposal](openspec/changes/audio-recording/proposal.md): In-browser audio recording via MediaRecorder API, upload to existing endpoint, automatic transcription with TranscribeAudioJob
- [Audio Recording Design](openspec/changes/audio-recording/design.md): Architecture overview, Stimulus controller (audio_recorder_controller.js), UI placement, CSS for recording indicator, backend upload endpoint enhancement, supported audio formats, error handling
- [Audio Recording Tasks](openspec/changes/audio-recording/tasks.md): Implementation task breakdown -- backend upload endpoint triggers transcription, frontend Stimulus controller, testing phases

## Source Code

- [Routes](config/routes.rb): Rails routing configuration
- [Database Schema](db/schema.rb): Current database schema (SQLite3)
- [Gemfile](Gemfile): Ruby dependencies
- [Package JSON](package.json): Node.js dependencies
- [Docker Compose](docker-compose.yml): Container orchestration configuration
- [Dockerfile](Dockerfile): Application container build
- [Procfile.dev](Procfile.dev): Development process manager configuration
